sentences.zh: 易敘宓羲、神農、皇帝作教化民，而傳述其官，以為宓羲龍師名官，神農火師火名，黃帝雲師雲名，少昊鳥師鳥名。

translations.literal: The Changes narrates how Fuxi, Shennong, and the Yellow Emperor created teachings and transformed the people, and the tradition describes their officials: Fuxi named his officials after dragon masters, Shennong after fire masters, the Yellow Emperor after cloud masters, and Shaohao after bird masters.
translations.idiomatic: The Changes recounts how Fuxi, Shennong, and the Yellow Emperor instituted teachings to civilize the people. Tradition records that Fuxi named his officials after dragons, Shennong after fire, the Yellow Emperor after clouds, and Shaohao after birds.

sentences.zh: 自顓頊以來，為民師而命以民事，有重黎、句芒、祝融、后土、蓐收、玄冥之官，然已上矣。

translations.literal: From Zhuanxu onward, officials served as teachers of the people and were charged with the people's affairs; there were the offices of Chongli, Goumang, Zhurong, Houtu, Rushou, and Xuanming. But these are already matters of remote antiquity.
translations.idiomatic: From Zhuanxu onward, officials served as teachers of the people and were charged with the people's affairs. There were the offices of Chongli, Goumang, Zhurong, Houtu, Rushou, and Xuanming — but all this belongs to remote antiquity.

sentences.zh: 書載唐虞之際，命羲和四子順天文，授民時；

translations.literal: The Documents records that in the time of Tang and Yu, the four sons of Xi and He were charged with following the celestial patterns and conveying the seasons to the people;
translations.idiomatic: The Documents records that in the age of Yao and Shun, the four sons of Xi and He were charged with observing the heavens and conveying the seasons to the people;

sentences.zh: 咨四岳，以舉賢材，揚側陃；

translations.literal: the Four Peaks were consulted to raise up worthy and talented men from among the humble and obscure;
translations.idiomatic: the Four Peaks were consulted to elevate men of worth and talent from among the humble and obscure;

sentences.zh: 十有二牧，柔遠能邇；

translations.literal: the Twelve Shepherds nurtured those far away and brought harmony to those nearby;
translations.idiomatic: the Twelve Shepherds brought gentleness to the distant and harmony to those near at hand;

sentences.zh: 禹作司空，平水土；

translations.literal: Yu served as Minister of Works and tamed the waters and soil;
translations.idiomatic: Yu served as Minister of Works, taming the waters and ordering the soil;

sentences.zh: 棄作后稷，播百穀；

translations.literal: Qi served as Lord Millet and sowed the hundred grains;
translations.idiomatic: Qi served as Lord Millet, sowing the hundred grains;

sentences.zh: 镨作司徒，敷五教；

translations.literal: Xie served as Minister of Education and spread the five teachings;
translations.idiomatic: Xie served as Minister of Education, spreading the five moral teachings;

sentences.zh: 咎繇作士，正五刑；

translations.literal: Gao Yao served as Minister of Justice and administered the five punishments;
translations.idiomatic: Gao Yao served as Minister of Justice, administering the five punishments;

sentences.zh: 垂作共工，利器用；

translations.literal: Chui served as Minister of Public Works and improved tools and implements;
translations.idiomatic: Chui served as Minister of Public Works, improving tools and implements;

sentences.zh: 籄作朕虞，育草木鳥獸；

translations.literal: Yi served as my Forester and nurtured plants, trees, birds, and beasts;
translations.idiomatic: Yi served as Forester, nurturing the plants, trees, birds, and beasts;

sentences.zh: 伯夷作秩宗，典三禮；

translations.literal: Bo Yi served as Director of Ritual and oversaw the three rites;
translations.idiomatic: Bo Yi served as Director of Ritual, overseeing the three categories of rites;

sentences.zh: 夔典樂，和神人；

translations.literal: Kui oversaw music and harmonized spirits and humans;
translations.idiomatic: Kui oversaw music, harmonizing the spirits and humankind;

sentences.zh: 龍作納言，出入帝命。

translations.literal: Long served as Receiver of Words and conveyed the emperor's commands in and out.
translations.idiomatic: and Long served as Receiver of Words, conveying the emperor's commands in and out.

sentences.zh: 夏、殷亡聞焉，周官則備矣。

translations.literal: Of the Xia and Yin nothing is heard, but the offices of the Zhou were complete.
translations.idiomatic: Of the Xia and Yin offices, nothing survives on record. But the offices of the Zhou were comprehensive.

sentences.zh: 天官冢宰，地官司徒，春官宗伯，夏官司馬，秋官司寇，冬官司空，是為六卿，各有徒屬職分，用於百事。

translations.literal: The Heavenly Office of the Grand Steward, the Earthly Office of the Minister of Education, the Spring Office of the Grand Priest, the Summer Office of the Minister of War, the Autumn Office of the Minister of Justice, and the Winter Office of the Minister of Works — these constituted the Six Ministers. Each had subordinates, attached officials, and assigned duties, employed in all manner of affairs.
translations.idiomatic: The Heavenly Office of the Grand Steward, the Earthly Office of the Minister of Education, the Spring Office of the Grand Priest, the Summer Office of the Minister of War, the Autumn Office of the Minister of Justice, and the Winter Office of the Minister of Works — these were the Six Ministers. Each had subordinates, assigned duties, and specific functions, employed across all affairs.

sentences.zh: 太師、太傅、太保，是為三公，蓋參天子，坐而議政，無不總統，故不以一職為官名。

translations.literal: The Grand Tutor, Grand Mentor, and Grand Protector constituted the Three Dukes. They participated in government alongside the Son of Heaven, sitting in council to discuss affairs of state, with nothing beyond their purview. Therefore they were not named after any single function.
translations.idiomatic: The Grand Tutor, Grand Mentor, and Grand Protector made up the Three Dukes. They participated in government alongside the Son of Heaven, sitting in council to deliberate on affairs of state, with authority over all matters. For this reason, their titles were not derived from any single function.

sentences.zh: 又立三少為之副，少師、少傅、少保，是為孤卿，與六卿為九焉。

translations.literal: Three Junior posts were established as their deputies: the Junior Tutor, Junior Mentor, and Junior Protector. These were the Solitary Ministers, who together with the Six Ministers made nine.
translations.idiomatic: Three Junior posts were established as their deputies: the Junior Tutor, Junior Mentor, and Junior Protector — known as the Solitary Ministers. Together with the Six Ministers, they made nine.

sentences.zh: 記曰三公無官，言有其人然後充之，舜之於堯，伊尹於湯，周公、召公於周，是也。

translations.literal: The Record states that the Three Dukes had no fixed office, meaning the positions were filled only when a suitable person existed — as with Shun under Yao, Yi Yin under Tang, and the Dukes of Zhou and Shao under the Zhou.
translations.idiomatic: The Record states that the Three Dukes held no fixed office — the position was filled only when the right person appeared. Such was the case with Shun under Yao, Yi Yin under Tang, and the Dukes of Zhou and Shao under the Zhou.

sentences.zh: 或說司馬主天，司徒主人，司空主土，是為三公。

translations.literal: Some say the Minister of War governed heaven, the Minister of Education governed the people, and the Minister of Works governed the earth — and these were the Three Dukes.
translations.idiomatic: An alternative tradition holds that the Minister of War governed heaven, the Minister of Education governed the people, and the Minister of Works governed the earth — and these constituted the Three Dukes.

sentences.zh: 四岳謂四方諸侯。

translations.literal: The 'Four Peaks' refers to the feudal lords of the four directions.
translations.idiomatic: The 'Four Peaks' refers to the feudal lords of the four quarters.

sentences.zh: 自周衰，官失而百職亂，戰國並爭，各變異。

translations.literal: When the Zhou declined, the offices were lost and the hundred functions fell into confusion. The Warring States competed with one another, each altering and innovating differently.
translations.idiomatic: When the Zhou declined, the offices were lost and the hundred functions fell into disarray. The Warring States competed against one another, each altering the system in its own way.

sentences.zh: 秦兼天下，建皇帝之號，立百官之職。

translations.literal: Qin unified the realm, established the title of Emperor, and set up the offices of the hundred officials.
translations.idiomatic: When Qin unified the realm, it established the title of Emperor and created the offices of the hundred officials.

sentences.zh: 漢因循而不革，明簡易，隨時宜也。

translations.literal: The Han followed and continued without reforming, keeping things clear and simple, adapting to the needs of the time.
translations.idiomatic: The Han followed the Qin system without reforming it, valuing clarity and simplicity, adapting to the circumstances of the time.

sentences.zh: 其後頗有所改。

translations.literal: Afterward there were considerable changes.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward, considerable changes were made.

sentences.zh: 王莽篡位，慕從古官，而吏民弗安，亦多虐政，遂以亂亡。

translations.literal: Wang Mang usurped the throne and aspired to follow the ancient offices, but officials and people were unsettled, tyrannical policies were widespread, and he ultimately perished in disorder.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Mang usurped the throne and sought to restore the ancient offices, but officials and people were unsettled. Tyrannical policies abounded, and he perished in the resulting chaos.

sentences.zh: 故略表舉大分，以通古今，備溫故知新之義云。

translations.literal: Therefore I have briefly tabulated the major divisions to connect past and present, fulfilling the principle of 'reviewing the old to understand the new.'
translations.idiomatic: And so I have briefly tabulated the major divisions, connecting past and present, in the spirit of 'reviewing the old to understand the new.'

sentences.zh: 相國、丞相，皆秦官，金印紫綬，掌丞天子助理萬機。

translations.literal: The Chancellor of State and the Grand Chancellor were both Qin offices. They bore gold seals with purple ribbons and were responsible for assisting the Son of Heaven in managing all affairs.
translations.idiomatic: The Chancellor of State and the Grand Chancellor were both Qin offices, bearing gold seals with purple ribbons. They were charged with assisting the Son of Heaven in managing all affairs of state.

sentences.zh: 秦有左右，高帝即位，置一丞相，十一年更名相國，綠綬。

translations.literal: Qin had both a Left and Right Chancellor. When Emperor Gao ascended the throne, he established a single Grand Chancellor. In the eleventh year the title was changed to Chancellor of State, with a green ribbon.
translations.idiomatic: Under Qin there were both Left and Right Chancellors. When Emperor Gao ascended the throne, he appointed a single Grand Chancellor. In the eleventh year, the title was changed to Chancellor of State, with a green ribbon.

sentences.zh: 孝惠、高后置左右丞相，文帝二年復置一丞相。

translations.literal: Under Emperor Hui and Empress Dowager Gao, Left and Right Grand Chancellors were established. In the second year of Emperor Wen, a single Grand Chancellor was restored.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Hui and Empress Dowager Gao, Left and Right Grand Chancellors were appointed. In the second year of Emperor Wen, the system returned to a single Grand Chancellor.

sentences.zh: 有兩長史，秩千石。

translations.literal: There were two Chief Clerks, with a salary rank of one thousand shi.
translations.idiomatic: The office had two Chief Clerks, each ranked at one thousand shi.

sentences.zh: 哀帝元壽二年更名大司徒。

translations.literal: In the second year of Yuanshou under Emperor Ai, the title was changed to Grand Minister of Education.
translations.idiomatic: In Yuanshou 2 under Emperor Ai, the title was changed to Grand Minister of Education.

sentences.zh: 武帝元狩五年初置司直，秩比二千石，掌佐丞相舉不法。

translations.literal: In the fifth year of Yuanshou under Emperor Wu, the position of Director was first established, with a salary rank equivalent to two thousand shi, responsible for assisting the Grand Chancellor in exposing lawbreakers.
translations.idiomatic: In Yuanshou 5 under Emperor Wu, the office of Director was first established at a rank equivalent to two thousand shi, charged with assisting the Grand Chancellor in exposing illegality.

sentences.zh: 太尉，秦官，金印紫綬，掌武事。

translations.literal: The Grand Commandant was a Qin office, bearing a gold seal with purple ribbon, responsible for military affairs.
translations.idiomatic: The Grand Commandant was a Qin office, bearing a gold seal with purple ribbon, in charge of military affairs.

sentences.zh: 武帝建元二年省。

translations.literal: In the second year of Jianyuan under Emperor Wu, the office was abolished.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianyuan 2 under Emperor Wu, the office was abolished.

sentences.zh: 元狩四年初置大司馬，以冠將軍之號。

translations.literal: In the fourth year of Yuanshou, the title Grand Marshal was first established, placed above the General's title.
translations.idiomatic: In Yuanshou 4, the title Grand Marshal was first established as a prefix to the General's title.

sentences.zh: 宣帝地節三年置大司馬，不冠將軍，亦無印綬官屬。

translations.literal: In the third year of Dijie under Emperor Xuan, a Grand Marshal was appointed without the General's prefix and without seal, ribbon, or subordinate officials.
translations.idiomatic: In Dijie 3 under Emperor Xuan, a Grand Marshal was appointed without the General prefix and without seal, ribbon, or subordinate staff.

sentences.zh: 成帝綏和元年賜大司馬金印紫綬，置官屬，祿比丞相，去將軍。

translations.literal: In the first year of Suihe under Emperor Cheng, the Grand Marshal was granted a gold seal with purple ribbon, given subordinate officials, a salary equal to the Grand Chancellor's, and the General title was removed.
translations.idiomatic: In Suihe 1 under Emperor Cheng, the Grand Marshal was granted a gold seal with purple ribbon, given subordinate staff, a salary matching the Grand Chancellor's, and the General title was dropped.

sentences.zh: 哀帝建平二年復去大司馬印綬、官屬，冠將軍如故。

translations.literal: In the second year of Jianping under Emperor Ai, the Grand Marshal's seal, ribbon, and subordinate officials were removed again, and the General title was restored as before.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianping 2 under Emperor Ai, the Grand Marshal's seal, ribbon, and staff were again removed, and the General prefix was restored as before.

sentences.zh: 元壽二年復賜大司馬印綬，置官屬，去將軍，位在司徒上。

translations.literal: In the second year of Yuanshou, the Grand Marshal was again granted a seal and ribbon, given subordinate officials, the General title was removed, and his position was placed above the Minister of Education.
translations.idiomatic: In Yuanshou 2, the Grand Marshal was again granted seal and ribbon, given subordinate staff, the General title dropped, and his rank placed above the Minister of Education.

sentences.zh: 有長史，秩千石。

translations.literal: There was a Chief Clerk, with a salary rank of one thousand shi.
translations.idiomatic: The office had a Chief Clerk ranked at one thousand shi.

sentences.zh: 御史大夫，秦官，位上卿，銀印青綬，掌副丞相。

translations.literal: The Grandee Secretary was a Qin office, ranked among the senior ministers, bearing a silver seal with blue-green ribbon, responsible for serving as the deputy of the Grand Chancellor.
translations.idiomatic: The Grandee Secretary was a Qin office ranked among the senior ministers, bearing a silver seal with blue-green ribbon, serving as deputy to the Grand Chancellor.

sentences.zh: 有兩丞，秩千石。

translations.literal: There were two Deputies, with a salary rank of one thousand shi.
translations.idiomatic: The office had two Deputies, each ranked at one thousand shi.

sentences.zh: 一曰中丞，在殿中蘭臺，掌圖籍祕書，外督部刺史，內領侍御史員十五人，受公卿奏事，舉劾按章。

translations.literal: One was called the Palace Deputy, stationed in the Orchid Terrace within the palace halls, responsible for maps, registers, and secret documents. Externally he supervised the Regional Inspectors; internally he commanded the fifteen Attendant Censors, received memorials from the ministers, and conducted impeachments and investigations.
translations.idiomatic: One was called the Palace Deputy, stationed at the Orchid Terrace within the palace. He oversaw maps, registers, and secret documents. Externally, he supervised the Regional Inspectors; internally, he commanded the fifteen Attendant Censors, received memorials from the ministers, and conducted impeachments and investigations.

sentences.zh: 成帝綏和元年更名大司空，金印紫綬，祿比丞相，置長史如中丞，官職如故。

translations.literal: In the first year of Suihe under Emperor Cheng, the title was changed to Grand Minister of Works, with a gold seal and purple ribbon, salary equal to the Grand Chancellor's, a Chief Clerk replacing the Palace Deputy, and the official duties remaining as before.
translations.idiomatic: In Suihe 1 under Emperor Cheng, the title was changed to Grand Minister of Works. He received a gold seal with purple ribbon and a salary matching the Grand Chancellor's. A Chief Clerk replaced the Palace Deputy, but the official functions remained unchanged.

sentences.zh: 哀帝建平二年復為御史大夫，元壽二年復為大司空，御史中丞更名御史長史。

translations.literal: In Jianping 2 under Emperor Ai, the title was restored to Grandee Secretary. In Yuanshou 2, it was changed back to Grand Minister of Works, and the Palace Deputy was renamed Chief Clerk of the Censorate.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianping 2 under Emperor Ai, the title reverted to Grandee Secretary. In Yuanshou 2, it was changed back to Grand Minister of Works, and the Palace Deputy was renamed Chief Clerk of the Censorate.

sentences.zh: 侍御史有繡衣直指，出討姦猾，治大獄，武帝所制，不常置。

translations.literal: Among the Attendant Censors there was the Embroidered Garment Direct Appointee, dispatched to suppress crafty and treacherous individuals and to handle major legal cases. This was instituted by Emperor Wu and was not a permanent position.
translations.idiomatic: Among the Attendant Censors was the Embroidered Garment Direct Appointee, sent out to suppress treachery and manage major cases. This was a creation of Emperor Wu and not a permanent position.

sentences.zh: 太傅，古官，高后元年初置，金印紫綬。

translations.literal: The Grand Mentor was an ancient office, first established in the first year of Empress Dowager Gao, with a gold seal and purple ribbon.
translations.idiomatic: The Grand Mentor was an ancient office first established in the first year of Empress Dowager Gao's reign, with a gold seal and purple ribbon.

sentences.zh: 後省，八年復置。

translations.literal: It was later abolished, then reestablished in the eighth year.
translations.idiomatic: It was later abolished, then reestablished in the eighth year.

sentences.zh: 後省，哀帝元壽二年復置。

translations.literal: It was abolished again, then reestablished in the second year of Yuanshou under Emperor Ai.
translations.idiomatic: It was abolished again, then reestablished in Yuanshou 2 under Emperor Ai.

sentences.zh: 位在三公上。

translations.literal: Its rank was above the Three Dukes.
translations.idiomatic: It ranked above the Three Dukes.

sentences.zh: 太師、太保，皆古官，平帝元始元年皆初置，金印紫綬。

translations.literal: The Grand Tutor and Grand Protector were both ancient offices, first established in Yuanshi 1 under Emperor Ping, with gold seals and purple ribbons.
translations.idiomatic: The Grand Tutor and Grand Protector were both ancient offices first established in Yuanshi 1 under Emperor Ping, with gold seals and purple ribbons.

sentences.zh: 太師位在太傅上，太保次太傅。

translations.literal: The Grand Tutor ranked above the Grand Mentor; the Grand Protector ranked below the Grand Mentor.
translations.idiomatic: The Grand Tutor ranked above the Grand Mentor, and the Grand Protector ranked just below.

sentences.zh: 前後左右將軍，皆周末官，秦因之，位上卿，金印紫綬。

translations.literal: The Generals of the Van, Rear, Left, and Right were all late Zhou offices, which Qin continued. They ranked among the senior ministers, with gold seals and purple ribbons.
translations.idiomatic: The Generals of the Van, Rear, Left, and Right were all late Zhou offices continued by the Qin. They ranked among the senior ministers, bearing gold seals with purple ribbons.

sentences.zh: 漢不常置，或有前後，或有左右，皆掌兵及四夷。

translations.literal: The Han did not maintain them permanently; sometimes Front and Rear were appointed, sometimes Left and Right. All were in charge of military affairs and the four barbarian peoples.
translations.idiomatic: The Han did not maintain them permanently — sometimes Front and Rear were appointed, sometimes Left and Right. All oversaw military affairs and relations with the four barbarian peoples.

sentences.zh: 有長史，秩千石。

translations.literal: There was a Chief Clerk, ranked at one thousand shi.
translations.idiomatic: Each had a Chief Clerk ranked at one thousand shi.

sentences.zh: 奉常，秦官，掌宗廟禮儀，有丞。

translations.literal: The Director of Ceremonies was a Qin office, responsible for ancestral temple rites and ceremonies, with a Deputy.
translations.idiomatic: The Director of Ceremonies was a Qin office in charge of ancestral temple rites and ceremonies, with a Deputy.

sentences.zh: 景帝中六年更名太常。

translations.literal: In the sixth year of the middle reign period under Emperor Jing, the title was changed to Grand Master of Ceremonies.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth middle year of Emperor Jing's reign, the title was changed to Grand Master of Ceremonies.

sentences.zh: 屬官有太樂、太祝、太宰、太史、太卜、太醫六令丞，又均官、都水兩長丞，又諸廟寢園食官令長丞，有廱太宰、太祝令丞，五畤各一尉。

translations.literal: Subordinate offices included six Directors and Deputies: Grand Music, Grand Invocator, Grand Steward, Grand Astrologer, Grand Diviner, and Grand Physician. There were also two Chiefs and Deputies: the Equalization Office and the Capital Water Office. Further, there were Directors, Chiefs, and Deputies for the various temple chambers, park provisioning offices, and the Grand Steward and Grand Invocator of Yong, with one Commandant for each of the Five Altars.
translations.idiomatic: Its subordinate offices included six Directors and Deputies — Grand Music, Grand Invocator, Grand Steward, Grand Astrologer, Grand Diviner, and Grand Physician. Additionally, the Equalization Office and Capital Water Office each had a Chief and Deputy. There were also Directors and Deputies for the temple chambers, park provisions, and the Yong Grand Steward and Grand Invocator, with one Commandant for each of the Five Altars.

sentences.zh: 又博士及諸陵縣皆屬焉。

translations.literal: The Erudites and all the imperial mausoleum counties were also under its jurisdiction.
translations.idiomatic: The Erudites and all the imperial mausoleum counties also fell under its jurisdiction.

sentences.zh: 景帝中六年更名太祝為祠祀，武帝太初元年更曰廟祀，初置太卜。

translations.literal: In Emperor Jing's sixth middle year, the Grand Invocator was renamed the Sacrificial Worship Director. In Taichu 1 under Emperor Wu, it was renamed the Temple Worship Director, and the Grand Diviner was first established.
translations.idiomatic: In Emperor Jing's sixth middle year, the Grand Invocator was renamed Director of Sacrificial Worship. In Taichu 1 under Emperor Wu, it became the Director of Temple Worship, and the Grand Diviner was first established.

sentences.zh: 博士，秦官，掌通古今，秩比六百石，員多至數十人。

translations.literal: The Erudites were a Qin office, responsible for expertise in ancient and modern affairs, ranked at the equivalent of six hundred shi, with a complement rising to several dozen.
translations.idiomatic: The Erudites were a Qin office responsible for mastery of ancient and modern learning, ranked at the equivalent of six hundred shi. Their number sometimes reached several dozen.

sentences.zh: 武帝建元五年初置五經博士，宣帝黃龍元年稍增員十二人。

translations.literal: In Jianyuan 5 under Emperor Wu, the Erudites of the Five Classics were first established. In Huanglong 1 under Emperor Xuan, the complement was gradually increased to twelve.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianyuan 5 under Emperor Wu, the Erudites of the Five Classics were first established. In Huanglong 1 under Emperor Xuan, their number was gradually increased to twelve.

sentences.zh: 元帝永光元年分諸陵邑屬三輔。

translations.literal: In Yongguang 1 under Emperor Yuan, the various mausoleum towns were separated and placed under the Three Adjuncts.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongguang 1 under Emperor Yuan, the mausoleum towns were transferred to the jurisdiction of the Three Adjuncts.

sentences.zh: 王莽改太常曰秩宗。

translations.literal: Wang Mang renamed the Grand Master of Ceremonies the Director of Ranks.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Mang renamed the Grand Master of Ceremonies to Director of Ranks.

sentences.zh: 郎中令，秦官，掌宮殿掖門戶，有丞。

translations.literal: The Director of the Gentlemen of the Palace was a Qin office, responsible for the palace halls and side gates, with a Deputy.
translations.idiomatic: The Director of the Gentlemen of the Palace was a Qin office in charge of the palace halls and side gates, with a Deputy.

sentences.zh: 武帝太初元年更名光祿勳。

translations.literal: In Taichu 1 under Emperor Wu, the title was changed to Superintendent of the Imperial Household.
translations.idiomatic: In Taichu 1 under Emperor Wu, the title was changed to Superintendent of the Imperial Household.

sentences.zh: 屬官有大夫、郎、謁者，皆秦官。

translations.literal: Subordinate officials included the Grandees, Gentlemen, and Ushers, all Qin offices.
translations.idiomatic: Its subordinate offices included the Grandees, Gentlemen, and Ushers — all Qin offices.

sentences.zh: 又期門、羽林皆屬焉。

translations.literal: The Gate Guards and the Feathered Forest also fell under its jurisdiction.
translations.idiomatic: The Gate Guards and the Feathered Forest Guards also fell under its jurisdiction.

sentences.zh: 大夫掌論議，有太中大夫、中大夫、諫大夫，皆無員，多至數十人。

translations.literal: The Grandees were responsible for deliberation and discussion. There were Grand Palace Grandees, Palace Grandees, and Remonstrant Grandees — all without a fixed complement, sometimes numbering several dozen.
translations.idiomatic: The Grandees were responsible for deliberation and debate. They comprised Grand Palace Grandees, Palace Grandees, and Remonstrant Grandees, with no fixed complement — sometimes numbering several dozen.

sentences.zh: 武帝元狩五年初置諫大夫，秩比八百石，太初元年更名中大夫為光祿大夫，秩比二千石，太中大夫秩比千石如故。

translations.literal: In Yuanshou 5 under Emperor Wu, the Remonstrant Grandee was first established at the equivalent of eight hundred shi. In Taichu 1, the Palace Grandee was renamed Grandee of the Glorious Emolument at the equivalent of two thousand shi; the Grand Palace Grandee remained at the equivalent of one thousand shi as before.
translations.idiomatic: In Yuanshou 5 under Emperor Wu, the Remonstrant Grandee was first established at an equivalent rank of eight hundred shi. In Taichu 1, the Palace Grandee was renamed Grandee of the Glorious Emolument at two thousand shi; the Grand Palace Grandee remained at one thousand shi as before.

sentences.zh: 郎掌守門戶，出充車騎，有議郎、中郎、侍郎、郎中，皆無員，多至千人。

translations.literal: The Gentlemen were responsible for guarding the gates and, when dispatched, serving as chariot and cavalry escorts. They comprised Consultant Gentlemen, Palace Gentlemen, Attendant Gentlemen, and Gentlemen of the Palace, all without a fixed complement — sometimes numbering up to a thousand.
translations.idiomatic: The Gentlemen guarded the gates and, when dispatched, served as chariot and cavalry escorts. They comprised Consultant Gentlemen, Palace Gentlemen, Attendant Gentlemen, and Gentlemen of the Palace — with no fixed complement, sometimes numbering up to a thousand.

sentences.zh: 議郎、中郎秩比六百石，侍郎比四百石，郎中比三百石。

translations.literal: Consultant Gentlemen and Palace Gentlemen were ranked at the equivalent of six hundred shi; Attendant Gentlemen at four hundred shi; and Gentlemen of the Palace at three hundred shi.
translations.idiomatic: Consultant Gentlemen and Palace Gentlemen ranked at the equivalent of six hundred shi, Attendant Gentlemen at four hundred, and Gentlemen of the Palace at three hundred.

sentences.zh: 中郎有五官、左、右三將，秩皆比二千石。

translations.literal: The Palace Gentlemen had three Commanders: the Five Offices, Left, and Right, all ranked at the equivalent of two thousand shi.
translations.idiomatic: The Palace Gentlemen had three Commanders — Five Offices, Left, and Right — each ranked at the equivalent of two thousand shi.

sentences.zh: 郎中有車、戶、騎三將，秩皆比千石。

translations.literal: The Gentlemen of the Palace had three Commanders: Chariot, Gate, and Cavalry, all ranked at the equivalent of one thousand shi.
translations.idiomatic: The Gentlemen of the Palace had three Commanders — Chariot, Gate, and Cavalry — each ranked at the equivalent of one thousand shi.

sentences.zh: 謁者掌賓讚受事，員七十人，秩比六百石，有僕射，秩比千石。

translations.literal: The Ushers were responsible for receiving guests, assisting in ceremonies, and handling affairs. Their complement was seventy, ranked at the equivalent of six hundred shi, with a Chief Usher ranked at the equivalent of one thousand shi.
translations.idiomatic: The Ushers received guests, assisted in ceremonies, and handled affairs. Their complement was seventy, ranked at six hundred shi, with a Chief Usher ranked at one thousand shi.

sentences.zh: 期門掌執兵送從，武帝建元三年初置，比郎，無員，多至千人，有僕射，秩比千石。

translations.literal: The Gate Guards were responsible for bearing arms and providing escort. First established in Jianyuan 3 under Emperor Wu, they ranked equivalent to Gentlemen, had no fixed complement (sometimes reaching a thousand), with a Chief ranked at one thousand shi.
translations.idiomatic: The Gate Guards bore arms and provided escort. First established in Jianyuan 3 under Emperor Wu, they ranked equivalent to Gentlemen, with no fixed complement — sometimes reaching a thousand — and a Chief ranked at one thousand shi.

sentences.zh: 平帝元始元年更名虎賁郎，置中郎將，秩比二千石。

translations.literal: In Yuanshi 1 under Emperor Ping, they were renamed Tiger-Ben Gentlemen, and a Commander of the Palace Gentlemen was established at the equivalent of two thousand shi.
translations.idiomatic: In Yuanshi 1 under Emperor Ping, they were renamed Tiger-Ben Gentlemen, with a Commander ranked at two thousand shi.

sentences.zh: 羽林掌送從，次期門，武帝太初元年初置，名曰建章營騎，後更名羽林騎。

translations.literal: The Feathered Forest Guards provided escort, ranking below the Gate Guards. First established in Taichu 1 under Emperor Wu, they were originally named the Jianzhang Camp Cavalry, later renamed the Feathered Forest Cavalry.
translations.idiomatic: The Feathered Forest Guards provided escort, ranking below the Gate Guards. First established in Taichu 1 under Emperor Wu as the Jianzhang Camp Cavalry, they were later renamed the Feathered Forest Cavalry.

sentences.zh: 又取從軍死事之子孫養羽林，官教以五兵，號曰羽林孤兒。

translations.literal: Additionally, the sons and grandsons of soldiers who died in battle were taken and raised in the Feathered Forest, where they were officially trained in the five weapons. They were called the 'Feathered Forest Orphans.'
translations.idiomatic: Additionally, the sons and grandsons of soldiers killed in battle were adopted into the Feathered Forest and trained in the five weapons. They were known as the 'Feathered Forest Orphans.'

sentences.zh: 羽林有令丞。

translations.literal: The Feathered Forest had a Director and Deputy.
translations.idiomatic: The Feathered Forest had a Director and a Deputy.

sentences.zh: 宣帝令中郎將、騎都尉監羽林，秩比二千石。

translations.literal: Emperor Xuan decreed that the Commander of the Palace Gentlemen and the Cavalry Commandant should supervise the Feathered Forest, at a rank equivalent to two thousand shi.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Xuan appointed the Commander of the Palace Gentlemen and the Cavalry Commandant to oversee the Feathered Forest, each ranked at two thousand shi.

sentences.zh: 僕射，秦官，自侍中、尚書、博士、郎皆有。

translations.literal: The Chief was a Qin office; the Attendants-in-Ordinary, Masters of Documents, Erudites, and Gentlemen all had one.
translations.idiomatic: The title of Chief was a Qin institution. The Attendants-in-Ordinary, Masters of Documents, Erudites, and Gentlemen all had one.

sentences.zh: 古者重武官，有主射以督課之，軍屯吏、騶、宰、永巷宮人皆有，取其領事之號。

translations.literal: In ancient times military officials were esteemed, and there was a Master of Archery to supervise and assess them. Military garrison clerks, outriders, stewards, and the personnel of the Eternal Lane all had one, taking the title from its supervisory function.
translations.idiomatic: In ancient times, military officials were highly valued, with a Master of Archery to supervise and assess them. Military garrison clerks, outriders, stewards, and Eternal Lane palace staff all had such a post, the title deriving from its supervisory function.

sentences.zh: 衛尉，秦官，掌宮門衛屯兵，有丞。

translations.literal: The Commandant of the Guards was a Qin office, responsible for the palace gate guards and garrison troops, with a Deputy.
translations.idiomatic: The Commandant of the Guards was a Qin office in charge of the palace gate guards and garrison troops, with a Deputy.

sentences.zh: 景帝初更名中大夫令，後元年復為衛尉。

translations.literal: At the beginning of Emperor Jing's reign, the title was changed to Director of the Palace Grandees. In the first year of the Later period, it was restored to Commandant of the Guards.
translations.idiomatic: Early in Emperor Jing's reign, the title was changed to Director of the Palace Grandees. In the first year of the Later period, it was restored to Commandant of the Guards.

sentences.zh: 屬官有公車司馬、衛士、旅賁三令丞。

translations.literal: Subordinate offices included three Directors and Deputies: the Public Carriage Commandant, the Guard Soldiers, and the Traveling Warriors.
translations.idiomatic: Its subordinate offices included three Directors and Deputies: the Public Carriage Commandant, the Guard Soldiers, and the Traveling Warriors.

sentences.zh: 衛士三丞。

translations.literal: The Guard Soldiers had three Deputies.
translations.idiomatic: The Guard Soldiers office had three Deputies.

sentences.zh: 又諸屯衛候、司馬二十二官皆屬焉。

translations.literal: Additionally, the various garrison guards, commandants, and marshals — twenty-two offices in all — were all under its jurisdiction.
translations.idiomatic: Additionally, all twenty-two garrison guard and marshal offices fell under its jurisdiction.

sentences.zh: 長樂、建章、甘泉衛尉皆掌其宮，職略同，不常置。

translations.literal: The Commandants of the Guards for the Changle, Jianzhang, and Ganquan palaces each oversaw their respective palaces, with duties broadly similar, and were not permanently maintained.
translations.idiomatic: The Commandants of the Guards for the Changle, Jianzhang, and Ganquan palaces each oversaw their respective palaces. Their duties were broadly similar, and the positions were not permanent.

sentences.zh: 太僕，秦官，掌輿馬，有兩丞。

translations.literal: The Grand Coachman was a Qin office, responsible for carriages and horses, with two Deputies.
translations.idiomatic: The Grand Coachman was a Qin office in charge of carriages and horses, with two Deputies.

sentences.zh: 屬官有大廄、未央、家馬三令，各五丞一尉。

translations.literal: Subordinate offices included three Directors: the Grand Stables, the Weiyang Stables, and the Imperial Stables, each with five Deputies and one Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: Its subordinate offices included three Directors — Grand Stables, Weiyang Stables, and Imperial Stables — each with five Deputies and one Commandant.

sentences.zh: 又車府、路軨、騎馬、駿馬四令丞；

translations.literal: Also four Directors and Deputies: the Carriage Office, Road Carriages, Riding Horses, and Fine Horses;
translations.idiomatic: Additionally, four Directors and Deputies: the Carriage Office, Road Carriages, Riding Horses, and Fine Horses;

sentences.zh: 又龍馬、閑駒、橐泉、騊駼、承華五監長丞；

translations.literal: also five Supervisors and Chiefs with Deputies: Dragon Horses, Idle Colts, Tuoquan, Taotu, and Chenghua;
translations.idiomatic: five Supervisors with Chiefs and Deputies: Dragon Horses, Idle Colts, Tuoquan, Taotu, and Chenghua;

sentences.zh: 又邊郡六牧師菀令，各三丞；

translations.literal: also six Pasture Park Directors in the border commanderies, each with three Deputies;
translations.idiomatic: six Pasture Park Directors in the border commanderies, each with three Deputies;

sentences.zh: 又牧橐、昆蹄令丞皆屬焉。

translations.literal: also the Directors and Deputies of the Camel Pasture and the Kunti all fell under its jurisdiction.
translations.idiomatic: and the Directors and Deputies of the Camel Pasture and Kunti also fell under its jurisdiction.

sentences.zh: 中太僕掌皇太后輿馬，不常置也。

translations.literal: The Palace Grand Coachman oversaw the carriages and horses of the Empress Dowager; the position was not permanent.
translations.idiomatic: The Palace Grand Coachman oversaw the Empress Dowager's carriages and horses. The position was not permanent.

sentences.zh: 武帝太初元年更名家馬為挏馬，初置路軨。

translations.literal: In Taichu 1 under Emperor Wu, the Imperial Stables were renamed the Fermented Mare's Milk Stables, and the Road Carriages office was first established.
translations.idiomatic: In Taichu 1 under Emperor Wu, the Imperial Stables were renamed the Kumiss Stables, and the Road Carriages office was first established.

sentences.zh: 廷尉，秦官，掌刑辟，有正、左右監，秩皆千石。

translations.literal: The Commandant of Justice was a Qin office, responsible for criminal law and punishments, with a Chief, Left Monitor, and Right Monitor, all ranked at one thousand shi.
translations.idiomatic: The Commandant of Justice was a Qin office in charge of criminal law and punishments, with a Chief, Left Monitor, and Right Monitor, each ranked at one thousand shi.

sentences.zh: 景帝中六年更名大理，武帝建元四年復為廷尉。

translations.literal: In Emperor Jing's sixth middle year, the title was changed to Grand Judge. In Jianyuan 4 under Emperor Wu, it was restored to Commandant of Justice.
translations.idiomatic: In Emperor Jing's sixth middle year, the title was changed to Grand Judge. In Jianyuan 4 under Emperor Wu, it was restored to Commandant of Justice.

sentences.zh: 宣帝地節三年初置左右平，秩皆六百石。

translations.literal: In Dijie 3 under Emperor Xuan, Left and Right Assessors were first established, each ranked at six hundred shi.
translations.idiomatic: In Dijie 3 under Emperor Xuan, Left and Right Assessors were first established, each at six hundred shi.

sentences.zh: 哀帝元壽二年復為大理。

translations.literal: In Yuanshou 2 under Emperor Ai, the title was restored to Grand Judge.
translations.idiomatic: In Yuanshou 2 under Emperor Ai, the title reverted to Grand Judge.

sentences.zh: 王莽改曰作士。

translations.literal: Wang Mang renamed it the Maker of Gentlemen.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Mang renamed it 'Maker of Gentlemen.'

sentences.zh: 典客，秦官，掌諸歸義蠻夷，有丞。

translations.literal: The Director of Guests was a Qin office, responsible for all the submitted barbarian peoples, with a Deputy.
translations.idiomatic: The Director of Guests was a Qin office in charge of the submitted barbarian peoples, with a Deputy.

sentences.zh: 景帝中六年更名大行令，武帝太初元年更名大鴻臚。

translations.literal: In Emperor Jing's sixth middle year, the title was changed to Director of Grand Conduct. In Taichu 1 under Emperor Wu, it was changed to Grand Herald.
translations.idiomatic: In Emperor Jing's sixth middle year, it was renamed Director of Grand Conduct. In Taichu 1 under Emperor Wu, it became the Grand Herald.

sentences.zh: 屬官有行人、譯官、別火三令丞及郡邸長丞。

translations.literal: Subordinate offices included three Directors and Deputies: the Envoys, Interpreters, and Separate Fire, as well as the Chiefs and Deputies of the commandery lodges.
translations.idiomatic: Its subordinate offices included three Directors and Deputies — Envoys, Interpreters, and Separate Fire — as well as the Chiefs and Deputies of the commandery lodges.

sentences.zh: 武帝太初元年更名行人為大行令，初置別火。

translations.literal: In Taichu 1 under Emperor Wu, the Envoys were renamed the Director of Grand Conduct, and the Separate Fire office was first established.
translations.idiomatic: In Taichu 1 under Emperor Wu, the Envoy office was renamed Director of Grand Conduct, and the Separate Fire office was first established.

sentences.zh: 王莽改大鴻臚曰典樂。

translations.literal: Wang Mang renamed the Grand Herald to Director of Music.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Mang renamed the Grand Herald 'Director of Music.'

sentences.zh: 初，置郡國邸屬少府，中屬中尉，後屬大鴻臚。

translations.literal: Originally, the commandery and kingdom lodges were placed under the Privy Treasurer, then transferred to the Capital Commandant, and later placed under the Grand Herald.
translations.idiomatic: Originally, the commandery and kingdom lodges fell under the Privy Treasurer, then were transferred to the Capital Commandant, and later placed under the Grand Herald.

sentences.zh: 宗正，秦官，掌親屬，有丞。

translations.literal: The Director of the Imperial Clan was a Qin office, responsible for the imperial kinsmen, with a Deputy.
translations.idiomatic: The Director of the Imperial Clan was a Qin office in charge of the imperial kinsmen, with a Deputy.

sentences.zh: 平帝元始四年更名宗伯。

translations.literal: In Yuanshi 4 under Emperor Ping, the title was changed to Grand Priest of the Clan.
translations.idiomatic: In Yuanshi 4 under Emperor Ping, the title was changed to Grand Priest of the Clan.

sentences.zh: 屬官有都司空令丞，內官長丞。

translations.literal: Subordinate offices included the Director and Deputy of the Capital Minister of Works, and the Chief and Deputy of the Inner Officials.
translations.idiomatic: Its subordinate offices included the Director and Deputy of the Capital Minister of Works, and the Chief and Deputy of the Inner Officials.

sentences.zh: 又諸公主家令、門尉皆屬焉。

translations.literal: The Household Directors and Gate Commandants of all the princesses also fell under its jurisdiction.
translations.idiomatic: The Household Directors and Gate Commandants of all the princesses also fell under its jurisdiction.

sentences.zh: 王莽并其官於秩宗。

translations.literal: Wang Mang merged this office into the Director of Ranks.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Mang merged this office with the Director of Ranks.

sentences.zh: 初，內官屬少府，中屬主爵，後屬宗正。

translations.literal: Originally, the Inner Officials were under the Privy Treasurer, then transferred to the Director of Nobility Ranks, and later placed under the Director of the Imperial Clan.
translations.idiomatic: Originally, the Inner Officials fell under the Privy Treasurer, then were transferred to the Director of Nobility Ranks, and later placed under the Director of the Imperial Clan.

sentences.zh: 治粟內史，秦官，掌穀貨，有兩丞。

translations.literal: The Internal Clerk for Grain was a Qin office, responsible for grain and commodities, with two Deputies.
translations.idiomatic: The Internal Clerk for Grain was a Qin office in charge of grain and commodities, with two Deputies.

sentences.zh: 景帝後元年更名大農令，武帝太初元年更名大司農。

translations.literal: In the first year of Emperor Jing's Later period, the title was changed to Director of Grand Agriculture. In Taichu 1 under Emperor Wu, it was changed to Grand Minister of Agriculture.
translations.idiomatic: In the first year of Emperor Jing's Later period, the title became Director of Grand Agriculture. In Taichu 1 under Emperor Wu, it became Grand Minister of Agriculture.

sentences.zh: 屬官有太倉、均輸、平準、都內、籍田五令丞，斡官、鐵市兩長丞。

translations.literal: Subordinate offices included five Directors and Deputies: Grand Granary, Equitable Transport, Price Stabilization, Capital Treasury, and Ritual Field; and two Chiefs and Deputies: the Monopoly Office and the Iron Market.
translations.idiomatic: Its subordinate offices included five Directors and Deputies — Grand Granary, Equitable Transport, Price Stabilization, Capital Treasury, and Ritual Field — and two Chiefs and Deputies: the Monopoly Office and the Iron Market.

sentences.zh: 又郡國諸倉農監、都水六十五官長丞皆屬焉。

translations.literal: Additionally, the various granaries, agricultural supervisors, and capital water officials of the commanderies and kingdoms — sixty-five Chiefs and Deputies in all — were all under its jurisdiction.
translations.idiomatic: Additionally, all sixty-five Chiefs and Deputies of the commandery and kingdom granaries, agricultural supervisors, and water offices fell under its jurisdiction.

sentences.zh: 騪粟都尉，武帝軍官，不常置。

translations.literal: The Grain Transport Commandant was a military office under Emperor Wu, not permanently maintained.
translations.idiomatic: The Grain Transport Commandant was a military office of Emperor Wu's era, not permanently maintained.

sentences.zh: 王莽改大司農曰羲和，後更為納言。

translations.literal: Wang Mang renamed the Grand Minister of Agriculture to Xi He, and later changed it again to Receiver of Words.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Mang renamed the Grand Minister of Agriculture 'Xi He,' later changing it again to 'Receiver of Words.'

sentences.zh: 初，斡官屬少府，中屬主爵，後屬大司農。

translations.literal: Originally, the Monopoly Office was under the Privy Treasurer, then transferred to the Director of Nobility Ranks, and later placed under the Grand Minister of Agriculture.
translations.idiomatic: Originally, the Monopoly Office fell under the Privy Treasurer, then was transferred to the Director of Nobility Ranks, and later to the Grand Minister of Agriculture.

sentences.zh: 少府，秦官，掌山海池澤之稅，以給共養，有六丞。

translations.literal: The Privy Treasurer was a Qin office, responsible for the taxes on mountains, seas, ponds, and marshes, used to supply the imperial household's needs, with six Deputies.
translations.idiomatic: The Privy Treasurer was a Qin office in charge of taxes on mountains, seas, ponds, and marshlands, used to supply the imperial household. It had six Deputies.

sentences.zh: 屬官有尚書、符節、太醫、太官、湯官、導官、樂府、若盧、考工室、左弋、居室、甘泉居室、左右司空、東織、西織、東園匠十二官令丞，又胞人、都水、均官三長丞，又上林中十池監，又中書謁者、黃門、鉤盾、尚方、御府、永巷、內者、宦者七官令丞。

translations.literal: Subordinate offices included twelve Directors and Deputies: the Masters of Documents, Tally and Credentials, Grand Physician, Grand Provisioner, Hot Water, Rice Processing, Music Bureau, Arsenal, Artisan Workshop, Left Archer, Dwelling Office, Ganquan Dwelling Office, Left and Right Ministers of Works, Eastern Weaving, Western Weaving, and Eastern Garden Artisans. There were also three Chiefs and Deputies: the Butchery, Capital Water, and Equalization offices. Additionally, the ten pond supervisors within the Shanglin Park, and seven Directors and Deputies: the Palace Secretary Ushers, Yellow Gate, Hook and Shield, Imperial Workshops, Imperial Wardrobe, Eternal Lane, Inner Attendants, and Eunuch Attendants.
translations.idiomatic: Its subordinate offices included twelve Directors and Deputies — Masters of Documents, Tally and Credentials, Grand Physician, Grand Provisioner, Hot Water, Rice Processing, Music Bureau, Arsenal, Artisan Workshop, Left Archer, Dwelling Office, Ganquan Dwelling Office, Left and Right Ministers of Works, Eastern Weaving, Western Weaving, and Eastern Garden Artisans. Three Chiefs and Deputies: Butchery, Capital Water, and Equalization. Ten pond supervisors within the Shanglin Park. And seven Directors and Deputies: Palace Secretary Ushers, Yellow Gate, Hook and Shield, Imperial Workshops, Imperial Wardrobe, Eternal Lane, Inner Attendants, and Eunuch Attendants.

sentences.zh: 諸僕射、署長、中黃門皆屬焉。

translations.literal: All the Chiefs, Bureau Heads, and Palace Yellow Gate officials also fell under its jurisdiction.
translations.idiomatic: All the Chiefs, Bureau Heads, and Palace Yellow Gate officials also fell under its jurisdiction.

sentences.zh: 武帝太初元年更名考工室為考工，左弋為佽飛，居室為保宮，甘泉居室為昆臺，永巷為掖廷。

translations.literal: In Taichu 1 under Emperor Wu, the Artisan Workshop was renamed the Artisans' Bureau, the Left Archer became the Swift Flyer, the Dwelling Office became the Palace Guardian, the Ganquan Dwelling Office became the Kunlun Terrace, and the Eternal Lane became the Lateral Court.
translations.idiomatic: In Taichu 1 under Emperor Wu, the names were changed: Artisan Workshop to Artisans' Bureau, Left Archer to Swift Flyer, Dwelling Office to Palace Guardian, Ganquan Dwelling to Kunlun Terrace, and Eternal Lane to Lateral Court.

sentences.zh: 佽飛掌弋射，有九丞兩尉，太官七丞，昆臺五丞，樂府三丞，掖廷八丞，宦者七丞，鉤盾五丞兩尉。

translations.literal: The Swift Flyer oversaw crossbow hunting, with nine Deputies and two Commandants. The Grand Provisioner had seven Deputies, the Kunlun Terrace five, the Music Bureau three, the Lateral Court eight, the Eunuch Attendants seven, and the Hook and Shield five Deputies and two Commandants.
translations.idiomatic: The Swift Flyer oversaw crossbow hunting, with nine Deputies and two Commandants. The Grand Provisioner had seven Deputies; the Kunlun Terrace, five; the Music Bureau, three; the Lateral Court, eight; the Eunuch Attendants, seven; and the Hook and Shield, five Deputies and two Commandants.

sentences.zh: 成帝建始四年更名中書謁者令為中謁者令，初置尚書，員五人，有四丞。

translations.literal: In Jianshi 4 under Emperor Cheng, the Director of the Palace Secretary Ushers was renamed Director of the Palace Ushers, and the Masters of Documents were first established with a complement of five and four Deputies.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianshi 4 under Emperor Cheng, the Palace Secretary Ushers' Director was renamed Director of the Palace Ushers, and the Masters of Documents were formally established with five members and four Deputies.

sentences.zh: 河平元年省東織，更名西織為織室。

translations.literal: In Heping 1, the Eastern Weaving office was abolished, and the Western Weaving was renamed the Weaving Chamber.
translations.idiomatic: In Heping 1, the Eastern Weaving office was abolished and the Western Weaving was renamed the Weaving Chamber.

sentences.zh: 綏和二年，哀帝省樂府。

translations.literal: In Suihe 2, Emperor Ai abolished the Music Bureau.
translations.idiomatic: In Suihe 2, Emperor Ai abolished the Music Bureau.

sentences.zh: 王莽改少府曰共工。

translations.literal: Wang Mang renamed the Privy Treasurer to Minister of Shared Works.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Mang renamed the Privy Treasurer 'Minister of Shared Works.'

sentences.zh: 中尉，秦官，掌徼循京師，有兩丞、候、司馬、千人。

translations.literal: The Capital Commandant was a Qin office, responsible for patrolling the capital, with two Deputies, a Scout, a Marshal, and a Commander of a Thousand.
translations.idiomatic: The Capital Commandant was a Qin office in charge of patrolling the capital, with two Deputies, a Scout, a Marshal, and a Commander of a Thousand.

sentences.zh: 武帝太初元年更名執金吾。

translations.literal: In Taichu 1 under Emperor Wu, the title was changed to Bearer of the Golden Mace.
translations.idiomatic: In Taichu 1 under Emperor Wu, the title was changed to Bearer of the Golden Mace.

sentences.zh: 屬官有中壘、寺互、武庫、都船四令丞。

translations.literal: Subordinate offices included four Directors and Deputies: the Central Rampart, Temple Mutual, Arsenal, and Capital Boats.
translations.idiomatic: Its subordinate offices included four Directors and Deputies: Central Rampart, Temple Mutual, Arsenal, and Capital Boats.

sentences.zh: 都船、武庫有三丞，中壘兩尉。

translations.literal: The Capital Boats and Arsenal each had three Deputies; the Central Rampart had two Commandants.
translations.idiomatic: Capital Boats and Arsenal each had three Deputies; the Central Rampart had two Commandants.

sentences.zh: 又式道左右中候、候丞及左右京輔都尉、尉丞兵卒皆屬焉。

translations.literal: Additionally, the Left, Right, and Central Scouts of the Ceremony Road and their Deputy Scouts, as well as the Left and Right Capital Adjunct Commandants and their Deputy Commandants and soldiers, all fell under its jurisdiction.
translations.idiomatic: The Left, Right, and Central Scouts of the Ceremony Road with their Deputies, and the Left and Right Capital Adjunct Commandants with their Deputies and soldiers, also fell under its jurisdiction.

sentences.zh: 初，寺互屬少府，中屬主爵，後屬中尉。

translations.literal: Originally, the Temple Mutual was under the Privy Treasurer, then transferred to the Director of Nobility Ranks, and later placed under the Capital Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: Originally, the Temple Mutual fell under the Privy Treasurer, then was transferred to the Director of Nobility Ranks, and later to the Capital Commandant.

sentences.zh: 自太常至執金吾，秩皆中二千石，丞皆千石。

translations.literal: From the Grand Master of Ceremonies to the Bearer of the Golden Mace, all were ranked at the middle two thousand shi level, and their Deputies were all ranked at one thousand shi.
translations.idiomatic: From the Grand Master of Ceremonies to the Bearer of the Golden Mace, all ranked at the middle two-thousand-shi level, with Deputies at one thousand shi.

sentences.zh: 太子太傅、少傅，古官。

translations.literal: The Heir Apparent's Grand Mentor and Junior Mentor were ancient offices.
translations.idiomatic: The Heir Apparent's Grand Mentor and Junior Mentor were ancient offices.

sentences.zh: 屬官有太子門大夫、庶子、先馬、舍人。

translations.literal: Subordinate officials included the Heir Apparent's Gate Grandees, Gentlemen of the Household, Outriders, and Retainers.
translations.idiomatic: Its subordinate officials included the Heir Apparent's Gate Grandees, Gentlemen of the Household, Outriders, and Retainers.

sentences.zh: 將作少府，秦官，掌治宮室，有兩丞、左右中候。

translations.literal: The Junior Treasurer of Construction was a Qin office, responsible for managing palace buildings, with two Deputies and Left, Right, and Central Scouts.
translations.idiomatic: The Junior Treasurer of Construction was a Qin office in charge of building the palaces, with two Deputies and Left, Right, and Central Scouts.

sentences.zh: 景帝中六年更名將作大匠。

translations.literal: In Emperor Jing's sixth middle year, the title was changed to Grand Architect.
translations.idiomatic: In Emperor Jing's sixth middle year, the title was changed to Grand Architect.

sentences.zh: 屬官有石庫、東園主章、左右前後中校七令丞，又主章長丞。

translations.literal: Subordinate offices included seven Directors and Deputies: Stone Repository, Eastern Garden Timber Master, and the Left, Right, Front, Rear, and Central School Commandants. There was also a Chief and Deputy of the Timber Master.
translations.idiomatic: Its subordinate offices included seven Directors and Deputies — Stone Repository, Eastern Garden Timber Master, and the five School Commandants (Left, Right, Front, Rear, and Central). There was also a Timber Master Chief with Deputy.

sentences.zh: 武帝太初元年更名東園主章為木工。

translations.literal: In Taichu 1 under Emperor Wu, the Eastern Garden Timber Master was renamed the Woodworkers.
translations.idiomatic: In Taichu 1 under Emperor Wu, the Eastern Garden Timber Master was renamed the Woodworkers' Bureau.

sentences.zh: 成帝陽朔三年省中候及左右前後中校五丞。

translations.literal: In Yangshuo 3 under Emperor Cheng, the Central Scout and the five Deputies of the Left, Right, Front, Rear, and Central Schools were abolished.
translations.idiomatic: In Yangshuo 3 under Emperor Cheng, the Central Scout and the Deputies of the five Schools were abolished.

sentences.zh: 詹事，秦官，掌皇后、太子家，有丞。

translations.literal: The Director of the Household was a Qin office, responsible for the empress's and heir apparent's households, with a Deputy.
translations.idiomatic: The Director of the Household was a Qin office in charge of the households of the empress and the heir apparent, with a Deputy.

sentences.zh: 屬官有太子率更、家令丞，僕、中盾、衛率、廚廄長丞，又中長秋、私府、永巷、倉、廄、祠祀、食官令長丞。

translations.literal: Subordinate offices included the Heir Apparent's Director of Timekeeping, Household Director and Deputy, Coachman, Central Shield, Guard Commander, and Kitchen-Stables Chief and Deputy; also the Director of the Inner Autumn, Private Treasury, Eternal Lane, Granary, Stables, Sacrificial Worship, and Provisions — all Directors, Chiefs, and Deputies.
translations.idiomatic: Its subordinates included the Heir Apparent's Director of Timekeeping, Household Director and Deputy, Coachman, Central Shield, Guard Commander, Kitchen-Stables Chief and Deputy; and also the Directors of the Inner Autumn, Private Treasury, Eternal Lane, Granary, Stables, Sacrificial Worship, and Provisions — each with Chiefs and Deputies.

sentences.zh: 諸宦官皆屬焉。

translations.literal: All eunuch officials also fell under its jurisdiction.
translations.idiomatic: All eunuch officials also fell under its jurisdiction.

sentences.zh: 成帝鴻嘉三年省詹事官，并屬大長秋。

translations.literal: In Hongjia 3 under Emperor Cheng, the Director of the Household was abolished, and its functions were merged under the Grand Director of the Autumn.
translations.idiomatic: In Hongjia 3 under Emperor Cheng, the Director of the Household was abolished, with its functions merged under the Grand Director of the Autumn.

sentences.zh: 長信詹事掌皇太后宮，景帝中六年更名長信少府，平帝元始四年更名長樂少府。

translations.literal: The Changxin Director of the Household oversaw the Empress Dowager's palace. In Emperor Jing's sixth middle year, the title was changed to Changxin Privy Treasurer. In Yuanshi 4 under Emperor Ping, it was changed to Changle Privy Treasurer.
translations.idiomatic: The Changxin Director of the Household oversaw the Empress Dowager's palace. In Emperor Jing's sixth middle year, it became the Changxin Privy Treasurer. In Yuanshi 4 under Emperor Ping, it became the Changle Privy Treasurer.

sentences.zh: 將行，秦官，景帝中六年更名大長秋，或用中人，或用士人。

translations.literal: The Director of Travel was a Qin office. In Emperor Jing's sixth middle year, the title was changed to Grand Director of the Autumn. The position was sometimes filled by eunuchs, sometimes by regular officials.
translations.idiomatic: The Director of Travel was a Qin office. In Emperor Jing's sixth middle year, it was renamed Grand Director of the Autumn. The post was sometimes held by eunuchs, sometimes by regular officials.

sentences.zh: 典屬國，秦官，掌蠻夷降者。

translations.literal: The Director of Dependent States was a Qin office, responsible for the submitted barbarian peoples.
translations.idiomatic: The Director of Dependent States was a Qin office in charge of submitted barbarian peoples.

sentences.zh: 武帝元狩三年昆邪王降，復增屬國，置都尉、丞、候、千人。

translations.literal: In Yuanshou 3 under Emperor Wu, when the Kunxie King surrendered, additional dependent states were established, with Commandants, Deputies, Scouts, and Commanders of a Thousand.
translations.idiomatic: In Yuanshou 3 under Emperor Wu, when the Kunxie King surrendered, additional dependent states were created, with Commandants, Deputies, Scouts, and Commanders of a Thousand.

sentences.zh: 屬官，九譯令。

translations.literal: The subordinate office was the Director of Nine Interpreters.
translations.idiomatic: Its subordinate office was the Director of Nine Interpreters.

sentences.zh: 成帝河平元年省并大鴻臚。

translations.literal: In Heping 1 under Emperor Cheng, the office was abolished and merged into the Grand Herald.
translations.idiomatic: In Heping 1 under Emperor Cheng, the office was abolished and merged with the Grand Herald.

sentences.zh: 水衡都尉，武帝元鼎二年初置，掌上林苑，有五丞。

translations.literal: The Water Conservancy Commandant was first established in Yuanding 2 under Emperor Wu, responsible for the Shanglin Park, with five Deputies.
translations.idiomatic: The Water Conservancy Commandant was first established in Yuanding 2 under Emperor Wu. It oversaw the Shanglin Park and had five Deputies.

sentences.zh: 屬官有上林、均輸、御羞、禁圃、輯濯、鍾官、技巧、六廄、辯銅九官令丞。

translations.literal: Subordinate offices included nine Directors and Deputies: Shanglin, Equitable Transport, Imperial Delicacies, Forbidden Garden, Wash and Repair, Bell Office, Artisan Skills, Six Stables, and Bronze Assessment.
translations.idiomatic: Its subordinate offices included nine Directors and Deputies: Shanglin, Equitable Transport, Imperial Delicacies, Forbidden Garden, Wash and Repair, Bell Office, Artisan Skills, Six Stables, and Bronze Assessment.

sentences.zh: 又衡官、水司空、都水、農倉，又甘泉上林、都水七官長丞皆屬焉。

translations.literal: Also the Balance Office, Water Minister of Works, Capital Water, Agricultural Granary, and the Ganquan Shanglin and Capital Water — seven Chiefs and Deputies in all — were under its jurisdiction.
translations.idiomatic: The Balance Office, Water Minister of Works, Capital Water, Agricultural Granary, and the Ganquan Shanglin and Capital Water — seven offices in all — also fell under its jurisdiction.

sentences.zh: 上林有八丞十二尉，均輸四丞，御羞兩丞，都水三丞，禁圃兩尉，甘泉上林四丞。

translations.literal: Shanglin had eight Deputies and twelve Commandants; Equitable Transport four Deputies; Imperial Delicacies two; Capital Water three; Forbidden Garden two Commandants; Ganquan Shanglin four Deputies.
translations.idiomatic: Shanglin had eight Deputies and twelve Commandants, Equitable Transport had four Deputies, Imperial Delicacies two, Capital Water three, Forbidden Garden two Commandants, and Ganquan Shanglin four Deputies.

sentences.zh: 成帝建始二年省技巧、六廄官。

translations.literal: In Jianshi 2 under Emperor Cheng, the Artisan Skills and Six Stables offices were abolished.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianshi 2 under Emperor Cheng, the Artisan Skills and Six Stables offices were abolished.

sentences.zh: 王莽改水衡都尉曰予虞。

translations.literal: Wang Mang renamed the Water Conservancy Commandant to 'Bestowed Forester.'
translations.idiomatic: Wang Mang renamed the Water Conservancy Commandant 'Bestowed Forester.'

sentences.zh: 初，御羞、上林、衡官及鑄錢皆屬少府。

translations.literal: Originally, the Imperial Delicacies, Shanglin, Balance Office, and Mint all fell under the Privy Treasurer.
translations.idiomatic: Originally, Imperial Delicacies, Shanglin, the Balance Office, and the Mint all fell under the Privy Treasurer.

sentences.zh: 內史，周官，秦因之，掌治京師。

translations.literal: The Internal Clerk was a Zhou office, continued by Qin, responsible for governing the capital.
translations.idiomatic: The Internal Clerk was a Zhou office continued by the Qin, in charge of governing the capital.

sentences.zh: 景帝二年分置左內史。

translations.literal: In the second year of Emperor Jing, a Left Internal Clerk was separately established.
translations.idiomatic: In Emperor Jing's second year, a Left Internal Clerk was established as a separate post.

sentences.zh: 右內史武帝太初元年更名京兆尹，屬官有長安市、廚兩令丞，又都水、鐵官兩長丞。

translations.literal: The Right Internal Clerk was renamed the Governor of the Capital Region in Taichu 1 under Emperor Wu. Subordinate offices included two Directors and Deputies: the Chang'an Market and Kitchen, as well as two Chiefs and Deputies: Capital Water and Iron Office.
translations.idiomatic: In Taichu 1 under Emperor Wu, the Right Internal Clerk was renamed Governor of the Capital Region. Its subordinate offices included the Chang'an Market and Kitchen Directors and Deputies, and the Capital Water and Iron Office Chiefs and Deputies.

sentences.zh: 左內史更名左馮翊，屬官有廩犧令丞尉。

translations.literal: The Left Internal Clerk was renamed the Left Adjunct. Its subordinate offices included the Director, Deputy, and Commandant of the Granary Sacrifices.
translations.idiomatic: The Left Internal Clerk was renamed the Left Adjunct. Its subordinate offices included the Director, Deputy, and Commandant of the Granary Sacrifices.

sentences.zh: 又左都水、鐵官、雲壘、長安四市四長丞皆屬焉。

translations.literal: Also the Left Capital Water, Iron Office, Cloud Rampart, and the Four Markets of Chang'an — four Chiefs and Deputies — all fell under its jurisdiction.
translations.idiomatic: The Left Capital Water, Iron Office, Cloud Rampart, and Four Markets of Chang'an — each with Chief and Deputy — also fell under its jurisdiction.

sentences.zh: 主爵中尉，秦官，掌列侯。

translations.literal: The Central Commandant of Nobility Ranks was a Qin office, responsible for the ranked marquises.
translations.idiomatic: The Central Commandant of Nobility Ranks was a Qin office in charge of the ranked marquises.

sentences.zh: 景帝中六年更名都尉，武帝太初元年更名右扶風，治內史右地。

translations.literal: In Emperor Jing's sixth middle year, the title was changed to Commandant. In Taichu 1 under Emperor Wu, it was renamed Right Adjunct, governing the right territory of the former Internal Clerk.
translations.idiomatic: In Emperor Jing's sixth middle year, it was renamed Commandant. In Taichu 1 under Emperor Wu, it became the Right Adjunct, governing the right portion of the former Internal Clerk's territory.

sentences.zh: 屬官有掌畜令丞。

translations.literal: Its subordinate offices included the Director and Deputy of Livestock Management.
translations.idiomatic: Its subordinate offices included the Director and Deputy of Livestock Management.

sentences.zh: 又有都水、鐵官、廄、廱廚四長丞皆屬焉。

translations.literal: Also the Capital Water, Iron Office, Stables, and Yong Kitchen — four Chiefs and Deputies — all fell under its jurisdiction.
translations.idiomatic: The Capital Water, Iron Office, Stables, and Yong Kitchen, each with Chief and Deputy, also fell under its jurisdiction.

sentences.zh: 與左馮翊、京兆尹是為三輔，皆有兩丞。

translations.literal: Together with the Left Adjunct and the Governor of the Capital Region, these constituted the Three Adjuncts, each with two Deputies.
translations.idiomatic: Together with the Left Adjunct and the Governor of the Capital Region, these formed the Three Adjuncts. Each had two Deputies.

sentences.zh: 列侯更屬大鴻臚。

translations.literal: The ranked marquises were subsequently placed under the Grand Herald.
translations.idiomatic: The ranked marquises were later transferred to the Grand Herald's jurisdiction.

sentences.zh: 元鼎四年更置二輔都尉、都尉丞各一人。

translations.literal: In Yuanding 4, two Adjunct Commandants and one Deputy Commandant each were additionally established.
translations.idiomatic: In Yuanding 4, two Adjunct Commandants with one Deputy each were additionally established.

sentences.zh: 自太子太傅至右扶風，皆秩二千石，丞六百石。

translations.literal: From the Heir Apparent's Grand Mentor to the Right Adjunct, all were ranked at two thousand shi, with Deputies at six hundred shi.
translations.idiomatic: From the Heir Apparent's Grand Mentor to the Right Adjunct, all ranked at two thousand shi, with Deputies at six hundred shi.

sentences.zh: 護軍都尉，秦官，武帝元狩四年屬大司馬，成帝綏和元年居大司馬府比司直，哀帝元壽元年更名司寇，平帝元始元年更名護軍。

translations.literal: The Protector-General Commandant was a Qin office. In Yuanshou 4 under Emperor Wu, it was placed under the Grand Marshal. In Suihe 1 under Emperor Cheng, it was stationed in the Grand Marshal's office at a rank equivalent to the Director. In Yuanshou 1 under Emperor Ai, it was renamed Minister of Crime. In Yuanshi 1 under Emperor Ping, it was renamed Protector-General.
translations.idiomatic: The Protector-General Commandant was a Qin office. In Yuanshou 4 under Emperor Wu it was placed under the Grand Marshal. In Suihe 1 under Emperor Cheng it was stationed in the Grand Marshal's office at Director-equivalent rank. In Yuanshou 1 under Emperor Ai it was renamed Minister of Crime, and in Yuanshi 1 under Emperor Ping it became Protector-General.

sentences.zh: 司隸校尉，周官，武帝征和四年初置。

translations.literal: The Colonel of the Capital Jurisdiction was a Zhou office, first established in Zhenghe 4 under Emperor Wu.
translations.idiomatic: The Colonel of the Capital Jurisdiction was a Zhou office first established in Zhenghe 4 under Emperor Wu.

sentences.zh: 持節，從中都官徒千二百人，捕巫蠱，督大姦猾。

translations.literal: Holding the imperial credentials, he commanded 1,200 convict laborers from the central capital offices to apprehend practitioners of witchcraft and sorcery and to supervise the most crafty and treacherous.
translations.idiomatic: Bearing the imperial credentials, he led 1,200 convict laborers from the capital offices to apprehend witchcraft practitioners and crack down on the most crafty and treacherous.

sentences.zh: 後罷其兵。

translations.literal: Later his troops were disbanded.
translations.idiomatic: Later, his troops were disbanded.

sentences.zh: 察三輔、三河、弘農。

translations.literal: He inspected the Three Adjuncts, the Three Rivers, and Hongnong.
translations.idiomatic: He inspected the Three Adjuncts, the Three Rivers region, and Hongnong.

sentences.zh: 元帝初元四年去節。

translations.literal: In Chuyuan 4 under Emperor Yuan, the imperial credentials were removed.
translations.idiomatic: In Chuyuan 4 under Emperor Yuan, the imperial credentials were taken away.

sentences.zh: 成帝元延四年省。

translations.literal: In Yuanyan 4 under Emperor Cheng, the office was abolished.
translations.idiomatic: In Yuanyan 4 under Emperor Cheng, the office was abolished.

sentences.zh: 綏和二年，哀帝復置，但為司隸，冠進賢冠，屬大司空，比司直。

translations.literal: In Suihe 2, Emperor Ai restored the office as simply the Capital Jurisdiction Colonel, wearing the Advancing Worthies cap, under the Grand Minister of Works, with a rank equivalent to the Director.
translations.idiomatic: In Suihe 2, Emperor Ai restored the position as simply 'Capital Jurisdiction Colonel,' wearing the Advancing Worthies cap, under the Grand Minister of Works at Director-equivalent rank.

sentences.zh: 城門校尉掌京師城門屯兵，有司馬、十二城門候。

translations.literal: The Colonel of the City Gates oversaw the garrison troops at the capital's city gates, with a Marshal and twelve City Gate Scouts.
translations.idiomatic: The Colonel of the City Gates oversaw the garrison troops at the capital's gates, with a Marshal and twelve Gate Scouts.

sentences.zh: 中壘校尉掌北軍壘門內，外掌西域。

translations.literal: The Colonel of the Central Rampart oversaw the interior of the Northern Army's rampart gates, and externally oversaw the Western Regions.
translations.idiomatic: The Colonel of the Central Rampart oversaw the Northern Army's inner rampart gates and, externally, the Western Regions.

sentences.zh: 屯騎校尉掌騎士。

translations.literal: The Colonel of Garrison Cavalry oversaw the cavalrymen.
translations.idiomatic: The Colonel of Garrison Cavalry oversaw the cavalrymen.

sentences.zh: 步兵校尉掌上林苑門屯兵。

translations.literal: The Colonel of Infantry oversaw the garrison troops at the Shanglin Park gates.
translations.idiomatic: The Colonel of Infantry oversaw the garrison troops at the gates of the Shanglin Park.

sentences.zh: 越騎校尉掌越騎。

translations.literal: The Colonel of the Yue Cavalry oversaw the Yue Cavalry.
translations.idiomatic: The Colonel of the Yue Cavalry oversaw the Yue cavalrymen.

sentences.zh: 長水校尉掌長水宣曲胡騎。

translations.literal: The Colonel of Changshui oversaw the Hu cavalry of Changshui and Xuanqu.
translations.idiomatic: The Colonel of Changshui oversaw the Hu cavalry stationed at Changshui and Xuanqu.

sentences.zh: 又有胡騎校尉，掌池陽胡騎，不常置。

translations.literal: There was also the Colonel of the Hu Cavalry, overseeing the Hu cavalry of Chiyang; this position was not permanent.
translations.idiomatic: There was also a Colonel of the Hu Cavalry, overseeing the Hu cavalry at Chiyang. This was not a permanent position.

sentences.zh: 射聲校尉掌待詔射聲士。

translations.literal: The Colonel of Archers Who Shoot by Sound oversaw the standby archers who shoot by sound.
translations.idiomatic: The Colonel of Archers Who Shoot by Sound oversaw the standby sound-shooting archers.

sentences.zh: 虎賁校尉掌輕車。

translations.literal: The Colonel of Tiger-Ben oversaw the light chariots.
translations.idiomatic: The Colonel of Tiger-Ben oversaw the light chariots.

sentences.zh: 凡八校尉，皆武帝初置，有丞、司馬。

translations.literal: In total there were Eight Colonels, all first established by Emperor Wu, each with a Deputy and a Marshal.
translations.idiomatic: In total there were Eight Colonels, all first established under Emperor Wu. Each had a Deputy and a Marshal.

sentences.zh: 自司隸至虎賁校尉，秩皆二千石。

translations.literal: From the Capital Jurisdiction Colonel to the Colonel of Tiger-Ben, all were ranked at two thousand shi.
translations.idiomatic: From the Capital Jurisdiction Colonel to the Colonel of Tiger-Ben, all ranked at two thousand shi.

sentences.zh: 西域都護加官，宣帝地節二年初置，以騎都尉、諫大夫使護西域三十六國，有副校尉，秩比二千石，丞一人，司馬、候、千人各二人。

translations.literal: The Protector-General of the Western Regions was an additional title, first established in Dijie 2 under Emperor Xuan. A Cavalry Commandant or Remonstrant Grandee was dispatched to protect the thirty-six states of the Western Regions. There was a Deputy Colonel, ranked at the equivalent of two thousand shi, with one Deputy, and two each of Marshals, Scouts, and Commanders of a Thousand.
translations.idiomatic: The Protector-General of the Western Regions was an additional title first established in Dijie 2 under Emperor Xuan. A Cavalry Commandant or Remonstrant Grandee was dispatched to protect the thirty-six Western Region states. The post included a Deputy Colonel ranked at two thousand shi, one Deputy, and two each of Marshals, Scouts, and Commanders of a Thousand.

sentences.zh: 戊己校尉，元帝初元元年置，有丞、司馬各一人，候五人，秩比六百石。

translations.literal: The Wuji Colonel was established in Chuyuan 1 under Emperor Yuan, with one Deputy, one Marshal, and five Scouts, ranked at the equivalent of six hundred shi.
translations.idiomatic: The Wuji Colonel was established in Chuyuan 1 under Emperor Yuan, with one Deputy, one Marshal, and five Scouts, each at six hundred shi.

sentences.zh: 奉軍都尉掌御乘輿車，駙馬都尉掌駙馬，皆武帝初置，秩比二千石。

translations.literal: The Commandant of Imperial Carriages oversaw the emperor's carriages; the Commandant of the Consort's Cavalry oversaw the consort's horses. Both were first established by Emperor Wu, ranked at the equivalent of two thousand shi.
translations.idiomatic: The Commandant of Imperial Carriages oversaw the emperor's carriages, and the Commandant of the Consort's Cavalry oversaw the consort's horses. Both were first established under Emperor Wu, at two thousand shi.

sentences.zh: 侍中、左右曹、諸吏、散騎、中常侍，皆加官，所加或列侯、將軍、卿大夫、將、都尉、尚書、太醫、太官令至郎中，亡員，多至數十人。

translations.literal: The Attendant-in-Ordinary, Left and Right Sections, Various Clerks, Loose Cavalry, and Regular Palace Attendant were all supplementary titles. Those receiving them ranged from ranked marquises, generals, ministers, and grandees to commanders, commandants, masters of documents, grand physicians, and grand provisioner directors down to gentlemen of the palace. There was no fixed complement, sometimes reaching several dozen.
translations.idiomatic: The Attendant-in-Ordinary, Left and Right Sections, Various Clerks, Loose Cavalry, and Regular Palace Attendant were all supplementary titles conferred on holders of other offices — from ranked marquises and generals down to gentlemen of the palace. There was no fixed complement, sometimes reaching several dozen.

sentences.zh: 侍中、中常侍得入禁中，諸曹受尚書事，諸吏得舉法，散騎騎並乘輿車。

translations.literal: The Attendants-in-Ordinary and Regular Palace Attendants were permitted to enter the inner palace; the Section officials received documents matters; the Various Clerks could initiate prosecutions; and the Loose Cavalry rode alongside the imperial carriage.
translations.idiomatic: Attendants-in-Ordinary and Regular Palace Attendants could enter the inner palace; Section officials handled documentary affairs; Various Clerks could initiate legal proceedings; and Loose Cavalry rode alongside the imperial carriage.

sentences.zh: 給事中亦加官，所加或大夫、博士、議郎，掌顧問應對，位次中常侍。

translations.literal: 'Palace Attendant' was also a supplementary title, conferred on grandees, erudites, or consultant gentlemen. It entailed serving as advisor and respondent, ranking next below the Regular Palace Attendant.
translations.idiomatic: 'Palace Attendant' was also a supplementary title, conferred on grandees, erudites, or consultant gentlemen. Its holder served as advisor and respondent, ranking just below the Regular Palace Attendant.

sentences.zh: 中黃門有給事黃門，位從將大夫。

translations.literal: The Palace Yellow Gate had 'Yellow Gate Palace Attendants,' ranking with the generals and grandees.
translations.idiomatic: Within the Palace Yellow Gate there were 'Yellow Gate Palace Attendants,' ranking among the generals and grandees.

sentences.zh: 皆秦制。

translations.literal: All of these were Qin institutions.
translations.idiomatic: All of these were Qin institutions.

sentences.zh: 爵：一級曰公士，二上造，三簪裊，四不更，五大夫，六官大夫，七公大夫，八公乘，九五大夫，十左庶長，十一右庶長，十二左更，十三中更，十四右更，十五少上造，十六大上造，十七駟車庶長，十八大庶長，十九關內侯，二十徹侯。

translations.literal: The ranks of nobility: first rank, Gongshi; second, Shangzao; third, Zanniao; fourth, Bugeng; fifth, Grandee; sixth, Official Grandee; seventh, Public Grandee; eighth, Public Rider; ninth, Fifth Grandee; tenth, Left Chief of the Multitude; eleventh, Right Chief of the Multitude; twelfth, Left Renewal; thirteenth, Central Renewal; fourteenth, Right Renewal; fifteenth, Junior Superior Constructor; sixteenth, Grand Superior Constructor; seventeenth, Four-Horse Chariot Chief of the Multitude; eighteenth, Grand Chief of the Multitude; nineteenth, Marquis within the Passes; twentieth, Full Marquis.
translations.idiomatic: The twenty ranks of nobility were: 1st, Gongshi; 2nd, Shangzao; 3rd, Zanniao; 4th, Bugeng; 5th, Grandee; 6th, Official Grandee; 7th, Public Grandee; 8th, Public Rider; 9th, Fifth Grandee; 10th, Left Chief of the Multitude; 11th, Right Chief of the Multitude; 12th, Left Renewal; 13th, Central Renewal; 14th, Right Renewal; 15th, Junior Superior Constructor; 16th, Grand Superior Constructor; 17th, Four-Horse Chariot Chief; 18th, Grand Chief of the Multitude; 19th, Marquis within the Passes; 20th, Full Marquis.

sentences.zh: 皆秦制，以賞功勞。

translations.literal: All were Qin institutions, used to reward merit and service.
translations.idiomatic: All were Qin institutions, used to reward merit and service.

sentences.zh: 徹侯金印紫綬，避武帝諱，曰通侯，或曰列侯，改所食國令長名相，又有家丞、門大夫、庶子。

translations.literal: Full Marquises had gold seals with purple ribbons. To avoid Emperor Wu's personal name taboo, the title was changed to 'Penetrating Marquis' or 'Ranked Marquis.' The Director or Chief of their alimentary domain was renamed Chancellor. They also had a Household Deputy, Gate Grandee, and Gentlemen of the Household.
translations.idiomatic: Full Marquises bore gold seals with purple ribbons. To avoid Emperor Wu's name taboo, they were called 'Penetrating Marquises' or 'Ranked Marquises.' The head of their alimentary domain was renamed Chancellor. They also had a Household Deputy, Gate Grandee, and Gentlemen of the Household.

sentences.zh: 諸侯王，高帝初置，金璽盭綬，掌治其國。

translations.literal: The feudal kings were first established by Emperor Gao, with gold seals and green ribbons, governing their own kingdoms.
translations.idiomatic: The feudal kings were first established by Emperor Gao, bearing gold seals with green ribbons, governing their own kingdoms.

sentences.zh: 有太傅輔王，內史治國民，中尉掌武職，丞相統眾官，群卿大夫都官如漢朝。

translations.literal: They had a Grand Mentor to assist the king, an Internal Clerk to govern the kingdom's people, a Capital Commandant in charge of military affairs, a Chancellor to direct all officials, and a full set of ministers, grandees, and capital officials modeled on the Han court.
translations.idiomatic: Each kingdom had a Grand Mentor to advise the king, an Internal Clerk to govern the people, a Capital Commandant for military affairs, and a Chancellor to direct all officials. The full set of ministers, grandees, and capital officials mirrored the Han court.

sentences.zh: 景帝中五年令諸侯王不得復治國，天子為置吏，改丞相曰相，省御史大夫、廷尉、少府、宗正、博士官，大夫、謁者、郎諸官長丞皆損其員。

translations.literal: In Emperor Jing's fifth middle year, the feudal kings were forbidden from governing their own kingdoms. The Son of Heaven appointed their officials. The Chancellor was renamed 'Chancellor' without the 'Grand' prefix, the Grandee Secretary, Commandant of Justice, Privy Treasurer, Director of the Imperial Clan, and Erudite offices were abolished, and the complements of the grandees, ushers, gentlemen, and all other officials were reduced.
translations.idiomatic: In Emperor Jing's fifth middle year, the feudal kings were barred from governing their own kingdoms. The Son of Heaven appointed their officials. The Chancellor's title lost the 'Grand' prefix, the offices of Grandee Secretary, Commandant of Justice, Privy Treasurer, Director of the Imperial Clan, and Erudites were abolished, and the complements of all remaining officials were reduced.

sentences.zh: 武帝��漢內史為京兆尹，中尉為執金吾，郎中令為光祿勳，故王國如故。

translations.literal: When Emperor Wu renamed the Han Internal Clerk to Governor of the Capital Region, the Capital Commandant to Bearer of the Golden Mace, and the Director of the Gentlemen to Superintendent of the Imperial Household, the kingdoms retained their old titles.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Wu renamed the Han Internal Clerk to Governor of the Capital Region, the Capital Commandant to Bearer of the Golden Mace, and the Director of the Gentlemen to Superintendent of the Imperial Household, the kingdoms retained the old titles.

sentences.zh: 損其郎中令，秩千石；
